title: Frontend performance containment and repaint coalescing
summary: Rendering work is bounded to visible rows plus a margin, cold large-buffer parsing is deferred, repeated lifecycle events are deduplicated, and scrolling repaints once after a burst.
details: util.visible_range limits normal decoration scans to the screen plus 40 rows; background buffers fall back to their whole content because no window range exists.
util.buf_lines shares a changedtick-keyed whole-buffer snapshot among consumers that genuinely need one.
Buffers with at least 400 lines enter a 40 ms cold gate: raw source paints immediately, Tree-sitter is warmed asynchronously, and one settled repaint decorates the stack.
treesitter-context is suspended across cold parsing so it cannot defeat the deferred first-paint budget.
util.on_decorate compares a paint signature including changedtick, cursor, viewport, mode, diagnostics generation, recording state, and cold state.
Cursor/mode events with unchanged text diff reveal sets and use row callbacks where available; horizontal moves that change no row presentation are skipped.
WinScrolled only arms a 100 ms timer; DansViewportSettled drives one repaint after scrolling stops.

provenance.note: Shared range, cold-open, cache, signature, reveal-delta, and scroll logic live here.
Participating modules and bespoke exceptions were mapped and interaction-tested.

evidence.claim: Incremental edits and cursor/mode transitions remain correct.
A reproducible 4437-line probe exercises cold, deferred, redraw, re-entry, and header paths; routine timings are not archived.

questions: Why does a large file show raw briefly on first open?
How is work bounded while scrolling?
Which state participates in decoration deduplication?

last_verified.method: Static lifecycle audit plus focused incremental/interaction specs and a disposable large-buffer probe.
